rxn_class_summary: photoredox-catalyzed dearomative [2π+2σ] cycloaddition of phenols with bicyclo[1.1.0]butanes to give bicyclo[2.1.1]hexane-fused cyclic enones

key_intermediates.name: benzylic/ring-opened radical intermediate III (common to both paths)

electron_flow: Visible-light-excited acridinium [PC1]* is reductively quenched by either the BCB (Path A, oxidation to radical cation I) or the phenol (Path B, oxidation to phenoxyl radical II); both pathways converge via Friedel–Crafts-type strain-release C–C bond formation to a common benzylic radical III, which undergoes 5-exo-trig cyclization to α-carbonyl radical IV, finally reduced by [PC1]•− to regenerate the photocatalyst and close the cycle.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: product 3a obtained in <5% yield, indicating involvement of radical intermediates

notes: Two mechanistic pathways are proposed and both are considered operative: Path A (oxidation of BCB 2a, E1/2 = 1.66 V vs SCE, thermodynamically slightly favored) and Path B (oxidation of phenol 1a, E1/2 = 1.70 V vs SCE, kinetically favored due to excess phenol). Stern–Volmer quenching shows BCB quenches [PC1]* ~1.4× more efficiently than phenol. Quantum yield Φ = 0.19 supports a closed catalytic (non-chain) cycle. UV–vis confirms PC1 is the only species absorbing at 450 nm, excluding direct substrate excitation.
